title: Hydrogen Fuel Cells Power First Zero-Emission Passenger Train on Metrolinkâ€™s Arrow Corridor
content: The San Bernardino County Transportation Authority (SBCTA) is rolling out North Americaâ€™s very first hybrid hydrogen fuel cells and battery-electric passenger train on Metrolinkâ€™s Arrow Corridor. Built by Stadler, the ZEMU (Zero Emission Multiple Unit) is a real game-changer for zero-emission technology in U.S. commuter rail â€” waving goodbye to diesel and steering us straight into sustainable energy territory.This is more than just a new train â€” itâ€™s a big step toward Californiaâ€™s ambitious decarbonization goals and a shining example of why industrial decarbonization in public transit makes sense. With the ZEMU on the tracks, SBCTA not only locks in federal and state dollars but also proves that these trains can beat diesel DMUs in cost efficiency. Plus, theyâ€™ve carved out a template with the Federal Railroad Administration that others can follow.Once ZEMUâ€™s been up and running, SBCTA will be swimming in real-world data on everything from reliability to fuel-cell longevity and upkeep costs. Those nuggets of info will be gold for other transit agencies plotting their next-gen fleets. Weâ€™re betting this pilot will kickstart a wave of investments in regional hydrogen production and a network of refueling stations â€” all thanks to the proof-of-concept right here in San Bernardino.
author: Analysts Account
